title: The Ambush at Ironwood Hall
description: A diplomatic envoy traveling under neutral-ground protection was ambushed inside the bounds of Ironwood Hall. The attackers were never officially identified. Durn Stoneshield was present as part of the Northern Reach escort and survived. The tradition of neutral ground that had held since the First Council of Kings was effectively ended. No subsequent council has been held at Ironwood Hall.
consequences: Ironwood Hall's neutral-ground status is broken
Diplomatic channels between Northern Reach and Compact collapse
War enters its most destructive phase
The question of who ordered the ambush is never officially resolved

title: The Burning of Ashveil
description: Ashveil — then a major city larger than Vareth — was destroyed in a single night. No army claimed responsibility. No siege was recorded. By dawn, only ash and silence. The cause remains unknown. The Wanderer was reportedly seen walking away from the smoke.
consequences: Destruction of a major population center
Permanently destabilized the southern coast
Created the current political vacuum in the Free Coast region

title: Collapse of the Compact
description: The Compact's governing council in Solmark fractured under accumulated war debt and internal blame. Three of the five founding houses withdrew their seals within a month of each other, citing irreconcilable accounts. What remained was not a government but a quarrel over ledgers. Vareth stopped sending any formal acknowledgment south.
consequences: Compact ceases to function as a unified political entity
Solmark survives as an independent city but loses its claim to regional authority
Northern Reach no longer has a formal counterpart to negotiate with

title: The Arrival of Eredan
description: The first recorded appearance of Eredan the Grey — entering the Known Lands from the northern highlands and making first contact with Lirien of the Vale. Contemporary accounts describe a meeting of several days in the Vale of Whispers, after which Eredan walked south toward Ironwood Hall and announced himself at the First Council.
consequences: Eredan enters the historical record
Lirien confirms his arrival was expected
The Vale of Whispers is marked as a significant location in founding texts

title: The Fall of the Grey Keep
description: The Grey Keep's garrison stopped responding to communications. A relief party found it empty — no bodies, no blood, no signs of struggle. The doors were locked from the inside. Aran Voss ordered it quietly reoccupied and the incident not discussed.
consequences: Grey Keep lost as a reliable outpost
Northern Reach northern flank exposed
Added to local folklore about cursed places

title: The First Council of Kings
description: The only recorded gathering of representatives from every known political entity in the Known Lands. Held in Ironwood Hall, which was chosen because every faction could agree it belonged to none of them. Eredan the Grey attended as an uninvited guest, offered three observations during the opening session, and was seated nonetheless. The Ironwood Accord defined the geographic boundaries that held until the Long Wars.
consequences: Formal recognition of political boundaries across the Known Lands
Ironwood Hall established as permanent neutral ground
Eredan the Grey enters official political records for the first time

title: The Founding of Morngate
description: Five trading families on the eastern ridge pooled resources to construct permanent walls around their settlement, drafting a founding charter that gave equal voice to each house. Unlike the Compact's later model, Morngate's charter explicitly included a clause dissolving the council if any single house accumulated majority control — a clause that was tested, and held, four times in the city's history.
consequences: Morngate established as a permanent fortified city
Eastern trade routes anchored to a fixed point for the first time
The eastern reaches gain a recognized civic center

title: Charter of the Compact
description: Five trading houses in Solmark signed the Charter of the Compact, creating a mutual defense and trade agreement. The Compact has no king — only the council and the ledger.
consequences: Created the Compact as a political entity
Established Solmark as a free city
Began the rivalry with Northern Reach

title: Founding of Vareth
description: The first permanent fortification on the Var River. Originally a watchtower controlling the northern passage through the Ashspine foothills. Grew into a city within two generations.
consequences: Established Northern Reach control over northern passage
Beginning of House Voss dynasty

title: The Free Coast Declaration
description: Port Maren's harbor council formally stopped sending tax shipments to any inland faction, citing no functioning authority to receive them. Drenford followed within the season. The coastal towns had effectively been self-governing for years; this was the declaration that made it official. No one arrived to contest it.
consequences: Coastal towns formally detach from any inland political claim
Port Maren consolidates as the dominant harbor on the Free Coast
Trade routes along the coast reorganize around Port Maren and Drenford

title: Lirien Leaves the Vale
description: Lirien of the Vale left the Vale of Whispers for the first time in recorded memory. A traveler who passed through the vale three days later found the standing stones unchanged and the single unnamed tree intact, but Lirien gone and her belongings absent. She was seen twice afterward — once on the road to the northern coast, once near the ruins of Ashveil — and then not again. No account of her destination exists.
consequences: The Vale of Whispers loses its only permanent resident
The end of an era of watchfulness that predates the Known Lands
Her destination and purpose unknown; this is deliberate

title: The Fall of Morngate
description: Morngate fell in the first year of the Post-Collapse — not by siege, not by starvation, but by an event that witnesses describe inconsistently. The walls remained standing. The guild records were untouched. Solan Vorne was present and survived. He does not discuss what he saw, and no official account has been filed. The city is now under the Pale King's influence, though what that means in practical terms varies by the district and the day.
consequences: Morngate falls under the Pale King's influence
Eastern trade routes collapse
Solan Vorne becomes one of the last independent witnesses of the event
The Known Lands enter genuine uncertainty about what the Post-Collapse means

title: The Emergence at Eastern Hold
description: Something arrived at Eastern Hold in the final years of the Long Wars. The garrison's reports changed in tone and content across three months, then stopped. When Kessa of Saltmere arrived on a routine delivery, she found the garrison still at their posts, the walls intact, the supply records perfectly maintained — and the garrison loyal to something other than the Northern Reach. She was the first outside person to observe this. She left without completing her delivery and rode to Morngate.
consequences: The Pale King establishes a presence in the Known Lands
Eastern Hold's allegiance permanently transferred
Morngate placed on high alert; no military response mounted
Kessa of Saltmere becomes the most credible witness to a phenomenon no one wants to officially acknowledge

title: The Road of the Wanderer
description: Eredan the Grey was seen walking the road between Ironwood Hall and Amber Fields in the nineteenth year of the Post-Collapse. He stopped at both places, spoke with those present, and continued north. He has not been reliably reported since. What he said at Amber Fields was recorded by a young farmer and preserved. What he said at Ironwood Hall was not written down — those present say they could not remember the words immediately after he left, only the feeling of having understood something.
consequences: Eredan exits the historical record
His words at Amber Fields become a foundational text in several local traditions
Ironwood Hall is visited by pilgrims seeking to stand where he stood
The nature of his departure — like his arrival — is unexplained

title: The Siege of Stonecrown
description: A Compact-aligned force besieged Stonecrown for sixty days in an attempt to control the western sea lanes. Durn Stoneshield, in his fourth year of command, held the fortress with a garrison of three hundred against a force of approximately two thousand. Eredan the Grey arrived on day forty-three and offered three pieces of advice. Durn took none of them. They were all correct. The siege was broken by a counter-raid on the attackers' supply column, which Durn planned and executed himself.
consequences: Stonecrown's reputation as impregnable is established
Durn Stoneshield elevated to senior Northern Reach command
Compact forces redirected from western approach
Eredan and Durn's peculiar friendship begins

title: The Siege of Vareth
description: The Compact's allied forces besieged Vareth for forty days. Aran Voss, twenty-three years old, had taken command of the city after his father's death on the third day. The siege was broken not by military force but by a flood that cut the supply lines. Both sides called it luck.
consequences: Aran Voss established as ruler of Vareth
Northern Reach survived as a political entity
The Compact retreated but was not defeated

title: The Defense of Amber Fields
description: A foraging column from a Compact-aligned mercenary band moved on Amber Fields during a supply shortage. Solan Vorne, then a courier attached to Morngate's garrison, was visiting his family. He organized the village's available men — twelve farmers, two retired soldiers, and a very angry miller — into a blocking force. The column retreated. Official records do not note the engagement. Solan was promoted twice in the following month, for reasons attributed to unrelated courier work.
consequences: Amber Fields spared from destruction
Solan Vorne's reputation established in Morngate's garrison
Solan promoted; his village connection becomes a liability he manages carefully

title: Thornwood Watch Abandoned
description: The last garrison at Thornwood Watch stopped receiving supply columns. They held position for two additional months on reserve stores, sent word south twice, and received no reply. On a morning in late autumn they extinguished the watch fires and marched back to Vareth. The post has been unmanned since.
consequences: Northern approach through the Thornwood is no longer monitored
The watch tower stands intact but dark
Locals treat the road through the Thornwood as safe by day, uncertain by night

title: The Treaty of Solmark
description: A temporary ceasefire negotiated through the merchant networks, not through official ambassadors. Mira Solhand is credited with arranging the initial meetings. Both sides signed and immediately began preparing to break it.
consequences: Temporary halt to active military conflict
Established secret trade routes between factions
Mira Solhand gained significant political influence
